questions.q: The Khilafat movement was launched mainly by which community in India?
questions.explanation: The Khilafat movement was started by Indian Muslims to defend the Ottoman Caliph.

questions.q: Around which year did the Khilafat movement begin?
questions.explanation: The Khilafat movement was launched around 1919 after the First World War.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement protested the treatment of which religious-political figure?
questions.explanation: It protested against the harsh treatment of the Ottoman Caliph after the war.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement allied with the campaign led by which Indian leader?
questions.explanation: The Khilafat leaders joined hands with Gandhi's nationalist campaign.

questions.q: What notable unity did the Khilafat movement briefly help forge?
questions.explanation: It forged a remarkable, though brief, unity between Hindu and Muslim political action.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement followed which major global conflict?
questions.explanation: It arose after the First World War, when the Ottoman Empire was being dismantled.

questions.q: The Caliph defended by the Khilafat movement was the head of which empire?
questions.explanation: The Caliph was the Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, also seen as a religious leader.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement is often paired in history with which other early 1920s movement?
questions.explanation: The Khilafat agitation was closely linked with Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement.

questions.q: The brothers most associated with leading the Khilafat movement were known as the what?
questions.explanation: Maulana Mohammad Ali and Maulana Shaukat Ali, the Ali Brothers, led the movement.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement was primarily a response to events affecting which empire's leader after the war?
questions.explanation: It responded to the postwar dismemberment of the Ottoman Empire and its Caliph.

questions.q: Who were the two brothers most prominently identified as Khilafat leaders?
questions.explanation: Maulana Mohammad Ali Jauhar and Maulana Shaukat Ali led the Khilafat agitation.

questions.q: Which 1920 treaty's harsh terms on the Ottoman Empire angered the Khilafat leaders?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Sevres imposed severe terms on Ottoman Turkey, inflaming Khilafat sentiment.

questions.q: Which prominent religious scholar and Congress leader supported the Khilafat cause?
questions.explanation: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad was a leading supporter of the Khilafat movement.

questions.q: At which 1919 session was the Khilafat cause formally organized in India?
options: The All-India Khilafat Conference at Delhi
questions.explanation: The All-India Khilafat Conference held at Delhi in 1919 formalized the movement.

questions.q: Gandhi linked the Khilafat issue with which other grievance to broaden the protest?
questions.explanation: Gandhi combined the Khilafat and Punjab (Jallianwala Bagh) wrongs to unite Indians.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement effectively ended after which leader abolished the Caliphate in Turkey?
questions.explanation: Mustafa Kemal Ataturk abolished the Caliphate in 1924, removing the movement's purpose.

questions.q: In which year was the Ottoman Caliphate abolished, undercutting the Khilafat movement?
questions.explanation: The Turkish Grand National Assembly abolished the Caliphate in 1924.

questions.q: The Hijrat of 1920, linked to Khilafat sentiment, involved Indian Muslims migrating to where?
questions.explanation: In the 1920 Hijrat, thousands of Muslims migrated toward Afghanistan, seen as a land of Islam.

questions.q: Which Congress session of 1920 endorsed cooperation with the Khilafat movement?
questions.explanation: The Nagpur Congress session of 1920 endorsed Non-Cooperation alongside the Khilafat cause.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement's alliance with Gandhi marked the entry of which group into mass politics?
options: Urban and rural Muslims in large numbers
questions.explanation: The movement drew large numbers of Muslims into the wider national struggle.

questions.q: Mohammad Ali Jauhar, a Khilafat leader, also founded which English-language newspaper?
questions.explanation: Mohammad Ali Jauhar founded and edited the newspaper Comrade.

questions.q: Which 1924 development in India, alongside the Caliphate's abolition, weakened the Khilafat movement?
questions.explanation: Rising communal tensions after Non-Cooperation, plus the Caliphate's abolition, broke the unity.

questions.q: Which Khilafat leader is buried in Jerusalem after his death in London in 1931?
questions.explanation: Mohammad Ali Jauhar died in London in 1931 and was buried in Jerusalem at his wish.

questions.q: The Khilafat Committee was formed in which Indian city in 1919?
questions.explanation: The Central Khilafat Committee was formed in Bombay in 1919 to coordinate the movement.

questions.q: Which poet-politician, author of the 'Inquilab Zindabad' slogan's popularization, was active in Khilafat circles?
questions.explanation: Maulana Hasrat Mohani, a Khilafatist, is credited with popularizing 'Inquilab Zindabad.'

questions.q: Which physician and nationalist served as a president of the Khilafat Conference and chaired Congress in 1921?
questions.explanation: Hakim Ajmal Khan was a Khilafat and Congress leader who presided over the 1921 Congress session.

questions.q: The Khilafat movement's collapse coincided with the suspension of which campaign after Chauri Chaura?
questions.explanation: Gandhi suspended Non-Cooperation in 1922 after Chauri Chaura, which also deflated the Khilafat alliance.

questions.q: Which Deoband-linked body lent religious backing to the Khilafat agitation?
questions.explanation: The Jamiat Ulema-e-Hind, founded in 1919, supported the Khilafat cause.

questions.q: The 1921 Moplah (Malabar) Rebellion is often linked to the agitation around which movement?
questions.explanation: The Moplah Rebellion in Malabar erupted amid Khilafat and Non-Cooperation agitation in 1921.

questions.q: Which Khilafat demand sought British protection of the Caliph's control over the holy places of Islam?
options: Preservation of the Jazirat-ul-Arab under the Caliph
A separate Muslim state in India
questions.explanation: Khilafatists demanded that the holy places (Jazirat-ul-Arab) remain under the Caliph's authority.
